zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Webb於1550年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Thomas Webb10歲，母親Barryce De Hall10歲。她於1580年與John Christopher Meals在英國英格蘭賀福爾萊奧明斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1610年在英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Webb于1550年出生在英国賀福爾时，她的父亲Thomas Webb10岁，母亲Barryce De Hall10岁。她于1580年与John Christopher Meals在英国英格兰賀福爾萊奧明斯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1610年在英国賀福爾萊奧明斯特享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Webb was born in 1550, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Thomas Webb, was 10 and her mother, Barryce De Hall, was 10. She married John Christopher Meals in 1580, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1610, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Webb est née en 1550, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Webb, avait 10 ans et sa mère, Barryce De Hall, avait 10 ans. Elle a épousé John Christopher Meals en 1580, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1610, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Webb 1550 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Webb 10 und ihre Mutter Barryce De Hall 10. Sie hat 1580 in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom John Christopher Meals geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1610 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Leominster, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Webb è nata il nel 1550 nel Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Webb, aveva 10 anni e sua madre, Barryce De Hall, aveva 10 anni. Lei sposato John Christopher Meals nel 1580, nel Leominster, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1610, nel Leominster, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Webb(이)가 1550년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Webb(은)는 10세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Barryce De Hall(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 1580년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 John Christopher Meals(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1610년 영국 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Webb родилась в 1550 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Webb было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Barryce De Hall, 10.
